info.name: Cekat Open API
info.description: Cekat Open API is designed to integrate your application with Cekat's features, such as creating WhatsApp message templates and sending WhatsApp messages. This API is specifically for integrating WhatsApp Business inboxes only.

item.name: Business

request.description:
This endpoint allows you to register a new business and get cekat open api key.

### Request Body

``` json
{
  "business_name": "business name",
  "email": "business email",
  "phone_number": "business phone number",
  "password": "password",
  "referral_code": "partner referral code",
  // below is for orderonline
  "user_id": "OO user ID",
  "personal_access_key": "OO personal access key"
}

 ```

### Response

The response is in JSON format and has the following schema:

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "status_code": 201,
    "cekat_api_key": "string api key"
}

 ```

item.description: User can register a business. After registering the business, user can also log in to the web chat at chat.cekat.ai by using the forget password feature. Through this API, user can also retrieve data of the registered businesses.

request.description:
### GET /inboxes

This endpoint retrieves a list of inboxes.

#### Request

There are no request parameters for this endpoint.

#### Response

The response is a JSON object with the following schema:

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "status_code": 200,
    "data": [
        {
            "id": "",
            "created_at": "2024-06-25T09:51:08.617927+00:00",
            "business_id": "",
            "name": "Cekat Development",
            "description": "Description",
            "phone_number": "628123456789",
            "status": "active",
            "ai_agent_id": "string or null",
            "image_url": "string or null",
            "type": "whatsapp"
        }
    ]
}

 ```

The response contains a boolean "success" flag, a "status_code" integer, and an array of "data" objects. Each "data" object represents an inbox with properties such as "id", "created_at", "business_id", "name", "description", "phone_number", "status", "ai_agent_id", "image_url", and "type".

### GET /templates

This endpoint retrieves templates associated with a specific business.

#### Request

- Method: GET
    
- URL: `{{OPENAPI_LOCAL_SERVER}}/templates`
    
- Query Parameters:
    
    - `inbox_id` (string, optional): The ID of the inbox for which templates are to be retrieved.
        
    - `status` (string, optional): The status which templates are to be retrieved.
        

#### Response

The response is a JSON object with the following schema:

``` json
{
    "success": boolean,
    "status_code": integer,
    "data": [
        {
            "id": string,
            "created_at": string,
            "body": string,
            "wa_template_id": string,
            "category": string,
            "file_url": string,
            "status": string,
            "language": string,
            "inbox_id": string,
            "name": string,
            "header_type": string,
            "header": string,
            "body_placeholder": [string],
            "inbox": {
                "id": string,
                "name": string,
                "type": string,
                "status": string,
                "business_id": string
            }
        }
    ]
}

 ```

The `data` array contains objects representing individual templates, with each object containing various properties such as `id`, `created_at`, `body`, `wa_template_id`, `category`, `file_url`, `status`, `language`, `inbox_id`, `name`, `header_type`, `header`, `body_placeholder`, and `inbox` details.

This endpoint allows you to send an HTTP POST request to synchronize WhatsApp templates from the Meta server to the Cekat server using the specified inbox ID. This synchronization is initiated when you want to add a template message from the Meta server. The request should include the inbox ID as a query parameter.

### Request Body

This request does not require a request body.

### Response Body

The response will be in JSON format with the following schema:

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "status_code": 200
}

 ```

formdata.value: Halo {{1}}, kamu apa kabarnya? Semoga baik-baik aja ya
formdata.description: (required) - The body content of the template.
(required) - The ID of the inbox associated with the template.
(optional or required) - placeholder for body variable

formdata.fileNotInWorkingDirectoryWarning: This file isn't in your working directory. Teammates you share this request with won't be able to use this file. To make collaboration easier you can setup your working directory in Settings.

request.description:
### Create Template

This endpoint allows the user to create a new template by making an HTTP POST request to the specified URL.

#### Form-Data Parameters

- `name`: (text, required) - The name of the template.
    
- `category`: (text, required) - The category of the template. The category must be one of the following: "AUTHENTICATION", "MARKETING", or "UTILITY". Typically, "MARKETING" is the most commonly used category.
    
- `language`: (text, required) - The language of the template. The language used should be a language code accepted by Meta, which includes the following:
    
    - Afrikaans: "af"
        
    - Albanian: "sq"
        
    - Arabic: "ar"
        
    - Azerbaijani: "az"
        
    - Bengali: "bn"
        
    - Bulgarian: "bg"
        
    - Catalan: "ca"
        
    - Chinese (CHN): "zh_CN"
        
    - Chinese (HKG): "zh_HK"
        
    - Chinese (TAI): "zh_TW"
        
    - Croatian: "hr"
        
    - Czech: "cs"
        
    - Danish: "da"
        
    - Dutch: "nl"
        
    - English: "en"
        
    - English (UK): "en_GB"
        
    - English (US): "en_US"
        
    - Estonian: "et"
        
    - Filipino: "fil"
        
    - Finnish: "fi"
        
    - French: "fr"
        
    - Georgian: "ka"
        
    - German: "de"
        
    - Greek: "el"
        
    - Gujarati: "gu"
        
    - Hausa: "ha"
        
    - Hebrew: "he"
        
    - Hindi: "hi"
        
    - Hungarian: "hu"
        
    - Indonesian: "id"
        
    - Irish: "ga"
        
    - Italian: "it"
        
    - Japanese: "ja"
        
    - Kannada: "kn"
        
    - Kazakh: "kk"
        
    - Kinyarwanda: "rw_RW"
        
    - Korean: "ko"
        
    - Kyrgyz (Kyrgyzstan): "ky_KG"
        
    - Lao: "lo"
        
    - Latvian: "lv"
        
    - Lithuanian: "lt"
        
    - Macedonian: "mk"
        
    - Malay: "ms"
        
    - Malayalam: "ml"
        
    - Marathi: "mr"
        
    - Norwegian: "nb"
        
    - Persian: "fa"
        
    - Polish: "pl"
        
    - Portuguese (BR): "pt_BR"
        
    - Portuguese (POR): "pt_PT"
        
    - Punjabi: "pa"
        
    - Romanian: "ro"
        
    - Russian: "ru"
        
    - Serbian: "sr"
        
    - Slovak: "sk"
        
    - Slovenian: "sl"
        
    - Spanish: "es"
        
    - Spanish (ARG): "es_AR"
        
    - Spanish (SPA): "es_ES"
        
    - Spanish (MEX): "es_MX"
        
    - Swahili: "sw"
        
    - Swedish: "sv"
        
    - Tamil: "ta"
        
    - Telugu: "te"
        
    - Thai: "th"
        
    - Turkish: "tr"
        
    - Ukrainian: "uk"
        
    - Urdu: "ur"
        
    - Uzbek: "uz"
        
    - Vietnamese: "vi"
        
    - Zulu: "zu"
        
- `body`: (text, required) - The body content of the template. To add variables with values that can be changed, use `{{1}}` for the first variable, `{{2}}` for the second variable, and so on.
    
    - body example with variables: "Halo {{1}}, terima kasih sudah melihat toko kami. Saat ini sedang ada diskon {{2}}%"
        
- `inbox_id`: (text, required) - The ID of the inbox associated with the template.
    
- `body_placeholder`: (text, required if body contains variables) - The placeholder values for the body content. If the template body contains variables, then `body_placeholder` becomes required. For example, if the body uses two variables, use a placeholder like `["Jane", "10"]`.
    
- `file`: (file, optional) - The file to be uploaded for the template. Currently, only image file formats are accepted:
    
    - jpeg
        
    - jpg
        
    - png
        

#### Response

The response is in JSON format with the following schema:

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "status_code": 201,
    "data": [
        {
            "id": "c814b87b-984c-4752-9ce5-0fa4917b80db",
            "created_at": "2024-06-27T01:03:24.664357+00:00",
            "body": "Halo {{1}}, terima kasih sudah melihat produk kami.",
            "wa_template_id": "123456789",
            "category": "MARKETING",
            "file_url": "string or null",
            "status": "PENDING",
            "language": "id",
            "inbox_id": "2824c651-57c9-43c1-8c8c-5d07dc40265b",
            "name": "terima_kasih",
            "header_type": "string or null,
            "header": "string or null,
            "body_placeholder": [
                "Jane"
            ],
            "inbox": {
                "id": "2824c651-57c9-43c1-8c8c-5d07dc40265b",
                "name": "Cekat Development",
                "type": "whatsapp",
                "status": "active",
                "business_id": "94879c90-d298-460f-8fb7-2b21b0916646"
            }
        }
    ]
}

 ```

### Delete Template

This endpoint is used to delete a specific template by providing its wa_template_id in the URL path.

#### Request Body

This request does not require a request body.

#### Response Body

Example Response:

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "status_code": 200
}

 ```

### Send Template

This endpoint allows you to send a template. Only template with status "APPROVED" will be proceed.

#### Request Body

- raw (application/json)
    

``` json
{
  "wa_template_id": "",
  "template_body_variables": [],
  "inbox_id": "",
  "otp_code": ""
  "phone_number": "",
  "phone_name": ""
}

 ```

#### Response

The response of this request is a JSON schema with the following structure:

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "status_code": 200,
    "data": [
        {
            "id": "",
            "created_at": "",
            "message": "",
            "sent_by": "",
            "status": "",
            "conversation_id": "",
            "media_url": null,
            "media_type": "",
            "is_deleted": false,
            "token_usage": null,
            "message_history": null,
            "system_msg": null
        }
    ]
}

 ```

### Send Template

This endpoint allows you to send a template. Only template with status "APPROVED" will be proceed.

#### Request Body

- raw (application/json)
    

``` json
{
  "wa_template_id": "",
  "template_body_variables": [],
  "inbox_id": "",
  "otp_code": ""
  "phone_number": "",
  "phone_name": ""
}

 ```

#### Response

The response of this request is a JSON schema with the following structure:

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "status_code": 200,
    "data": [
        {
            "id": "",
            "created_at": "",
            "message": "",
            "sent_by": "",
            "status": "",
            "conversation_id": "",
            "media_url": null,
            "media_type": "",
            "is_deleted": false,
            "token_usage": null,
            "message_history": null,
            "system_msg": null
        }
    ]
}

 ```

### Send Template

This endpoint allows you to send a template. Only template with status "APPROVED" will be proceed.

#### Request Body

- raw (application/json)
    

``` json
{
  "wa_template_id": "",
  "template_body_variables": [],
  "inbox_id": "",
  "otp_code": ""
  "phone_number": "",
  "phone_name": ""
}

 ```

#### Response

The response of this request is a JSON schema with the following structure:

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "status_code": 200,
    "data": [
        {
            "id": "",
            "created_at": "",
            "message": "",
            "sent_by": "",
            "status": "",

            "media_type": "",

            "token_usage": null,
            "message_history": null,

        }

}

 ```

### GET /inboxes

This endpoint retrieves a list of inboxes.

#### Request

There are no request parameters for this endpoint.

#### Response

The response is a JSON object with the following schema:

``` json
{
    "success": true,

        {

}

 ```

The response contains a boolean "success" flag, a "status_code" integer, and an array of "data" objects. Each "data" object represents an inbox with properties such as "id", "created_at", "business_id", "name", "description", "phone_number", "status", "ai_agent_id", "image_url", and "type".

item.name: New Open API

request.description:
# Get Item Detail

## Overview

Retrieves detailed information about a specific item from a CRM board. This endpoint returns comprehensive item data including basic item information, group details, and associated conversation data with contact information, inbox details, and pipeline status.

## Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `boardId` | string (UUID) | Yes | The unique identifier of the CRM board containing the item |
| `id` | string (UUID) | Yes | The unique identifier of the item to retrieve |

**Example:**

- `boardId`: `fc86ce1e-8be1-473d-99bf-c99e8869ae24`
    
- `id`: `42868389-0eaf-4110-93d6-8f6505a98669`
    

## Headers

| Header | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `access_token` | string | Yes | Authentication token for API access. Use `{{access_token}}` variable for dynamic token management |

## Response Structure

### Success Response (200 OK)

``` json
{
  "message": "success",
  "data": {
    "item_id": "string (UUID)",
    "item_name": "string",
    "group_id": "string (UUID)",
    "group_name": "string",
    "Test Conversation": {
      "id": "string (UUID)",
      "inbox": {
        "name": "string"
      },
      "contact": {
        "email": "string",
        "display_name": "string",
        "phone_number": "string"
      },
      "created_at": "string (ISO 8601 timestamp)",
      "handled_by": "string or null",
      "updated_at": "string (ISO 8601 timestamp)",
      "assigned_by": "string or null",
      "stage_status": "string",
      "last_active_node": "string or null",
      "assigned_ai_agent": "string or null",
      "pipeline_status_id": "string (UUID)"
    }
  }
}

 ```

### Response Fields

- **item_id**: Unique identifier for the item
    
- **item_name**: Display name of the item
    
- **group_id**: Unique identifier of the group this item belongs to
    
- **group_name**: Display name of the group
    
- **Test Conversation**: Conversation object associated with the item containing:
    
    - **id**: Unique conversation identifier
        
    - **inbox**: Inbox information where the conversation is located
        
    - **contact**: Contact details including email, display name, and phone number
        
    - **created_at**: Timestamp when the conversation was created
        
    - **updated_at**: Timestamp of the last update
        
    - **stage_status**: Current stage of the conversation (e.g., "resolved", "open", "pending")
        
    - **pipeline_status_id**: Reference to the pipeline status
        
    - **handled_by**: User handling the conversation (nullable)
        
    - **assigned_by**: User who assigned the conversation (nullable)
        
    - **assigned_ai_agent**: AI agent assigned to the conversation (nullable)
        
    - **last_active_node**: Last active workflow node (nullable)

item.description: # **Description** The **Cekat Open API** provides secure and structured access to your business data — including **inboxes**, **templates**, **campaigns**, **conversations**, and other connected resources. It allows developers and partners to integrate with the **Cekat platform** to manage customer interactions, synchronize data, and automate workflows. All endpoints in this collection are designed to be: - ✅ **Paginated** for efficient data retrieval. - ✅ **Filterable** by date range (`created_at_start`, `created_at_end`). - ✅ **Time zone–aware**, returning all timestamps in **Jakarta Time (GMT+7)**, which is **7 hours ahead of UTC**. - ✅ **Authenticated** using **Bearer Token** or **API Key**. # **Base URL** [https://api.cekat.ai](https://api.cekat.ai) # **Authentication** All requests require one of the following authentication methods: 1. **Bearer Token** `Authorization: Bearer` 2. **API Key** `api_key:` # #**Available API Endpoints** All endpoints use the base URL: [https://api.cekat.ai](https://api.cekat.ai) > 🕒 **Timezone:** All date and time filters use **Jakarta Time (GMT+7)** 🔐 **Authorization:** All endpoints support both `api_key` and `Bearer` token authentication. 📄 **Pagination:** Most endpoints include `page` and `limit` query parameters. | **Endpoint** | **Method** | **Description** | **Supports** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | `/api/messages` | **GET** | Retrieve paginated messages for a conversation. Supports filtering by conversation ID, message date, and ad messages. | Pagination, Date Filter, `is_ads`, `conversation_id` | | `/api/messages/summary/ai_credits` | **GET** | Get AI message usage summary (total AI messages, total credits used, total conversations). | Date Range Filter | | `/api/agents` | **GET** | Retrieve all agents within a business. Supports pagination and date filters. | Pagination, Date Filter | | `/api/inboxes` | **GET** | Get connected inboxes (WhatsApp, Instagram, Messenger, Livechat, etc.) for a business. | Pagination, Date Filter | | `/api/campaigns` | **GET** | Retrieve a paginated list of broadcast message campaigns created by the business. | Pagination, Date Filter | | `/api/campaigns/:id/messages` | **GET** | Get messages belonging to a specific campaign, including delivery and reply metrics. | Pagination | | `/api/agent_assignments` | **GET** | Retrieve which agent handled which conversation (assignment tracking). | Pagination, Date Filter | | `/api/conversations` | **GET** | Get a list of conversations between contacts and agents or AI. | Pagination, Date Filter | | `/api/contacts` | **GET** | Retrieve paginated contact data, including inboxes, platform type, and customizable additional data. | Pagination, Date Filter | | `/api/templates` | **GET** | Retrieve a list of message templates for a business (e.g., WhatsApp templates). | Pagination, Date Range Filter | ## #**Notes** - All timestamps and filters follow **Jakarta (GMT+7)** timezone. - Common query parameters for pagination: `?page=&limit=` - Common query parameters for date filtering: `?start_date=YYYY-MM-DD&end_date=YYYY-MM-DD` - { "success": true, "data": \[...\], "metadata": { "pagination": { ... }, "metrics": { ... } // (if applicable) }} # #**Time Zone Information** All `created_at` and `updated_at` fields are displayed in **Jakarta Time (GMT+7)**. For example: `Jakarta: 2025-10-09T08:00:00+07:00 -> UTC: 2025-10-09T01:00:00Z`

request.description:
### GET /business

This endpoint retrieves business information.

#### Request Body

This request does not require a request body.

#### Response

- Status: 200
    
- Content-Type: application/json
    

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "status_code": 200,
    "data": {
        "id": "",
        "created_at": "",
        "name": "",
        "email": "",
        "phone_num": "",
        "package_type": "",
        "package_start_date": "",
        "package_exp_date": "",
        "is_expired": false,
        "total_used_chat_credit_per_month": 0,
        "chat_credit_topup": 0,
        "total_used_convo": 0,
        "convo_credit_topup": 0,
        "open_api_key": ""
    }
}

 ```

The response contains a "success" flag, "status_code", and "data" object with various business details including ID, name, email, phone number, package information, expiration status, and credit usage.
